channel.title: Spinal Cord on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Spinal Cord on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Brain and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Nervous system disorders now the top cause of illness - March 2024
      • Brain problems reduced in both fetus and adult by Vitamin D – Oct 2022
      • Brain and Vitamin D chart - 2023
      • Microglia and Brain Disorders: The Role of Vitamin D and Its Receptor - July 2023
      • It appears that a fetus with adequate Vitamin D will have fewer brain problems
      • It appears that an adult with adequate Vitamin D will have fewer brain problems
      • VitaminDWiki - Brain development needs Vitamin D from week 3 thru 39
      • VitaminDWiki - How Vitamin D fights Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, Multiple Sclerosis and Epilepsy – June 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Central Nervous System protection by Vitamin D - Dec 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Cerebral Palsy and Vitamin D - many studies has
      • VitaminDWiki - Ensure a healthy pregnancy and baby - take Vitamin D before conception has:
      • VitaminDWiki - Cerebral Palsy and Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - Variety of evidence that Vitamin D helps the brain – July 2014
      • Study fails to mention that Vitamin D levels in cells can be much less than that in blood (gene problems)

Nervous system disorders now the top cause of illness - March 2024

Surging nervous system disorders now top cause of illness: study

item.title: Spinal cord injury - test Vitamin D bi-annually - 2022
item.description:
Chapter 35 - Vitamin D and spinal cord injury

in: Diagnosis and Treatment of Spinal Cord Injury - 2022, Pages 453-460

https://doi.org/10.1016/B978-0-12-822498-4.00035-X PDF is behind a paywall

Kelly Pritchett Brianna Rice Sophia Berg

Chapter Abstract

Vitamin D is a fat-soluble vitamin that plays a role in many functions in the body including musculoskeletal, immune, and neurological health. Vitamin D can be obtained through sun exposure, diet, and supplements; however, most Americans do not meet the recommended amount of vitamin D daily. Furthermore, the prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in the US is estimated to be around 40%. Athletes may be at an increased risk for vitamin D deficiency related to increased physical activity and enzymatic activity. Athletes with spinal cord injury (SCI) may be at even higher risk of vitamin D deficiency due to a lack of mobility, thermoregulatory dysfunction, and malabsorption concerns related to anticonvulsant medications.

item.title: Spinal Cord Injured persons were 7.6 X more likely to get pneumonia if low vitamin D
item.description:
Plasma Vitamin D, Past Chest Illness, and Risk of Future Chest Illness in Chronic Spinal Cord Injury (SCI): A Longitudinal Observational Study

Spinal Cord, 2020 Jan 16, PMID: 31949283 DOI: 10.1038/s41393-019-0409-7

Kristopher Clark 1, Rebekah L Goldstein 2, Jaime E Hart 3, Merilee Teylan 2, Antonio A Lazzari 1 4, David R Gagnon 5, Carlos G Tun 6, Eric Garshick 7 8

  • Spinal Cord Injury wheelchair athletes helped by 12 weeks of Vitamin D – Sept 2016

item.title: Spinal Cord Injury treated by Vitamin D progesterone combination, etc. – review of trials
item.description:
Neuroprotective Agents as an Adjuvant Treatment in Patients With Acute Spinal Cord Injuries: A Qualitative Systematic Review of Randomized Trials.

Clin Spine Surg. 2019 Aug 9. doi: 10.1097/BSD.0000000000000861.

Joaquim AF1, Daniel JW2, Schroeder GD3, Vaccaro AR4.

1 Department of Neurosurgery, State University of Campinas (UNICAMP), Campinas.

2 Neurosurgery, Santa Casa de São Paulo, São Paulo, SP, Brazil.

3 Department of Orthopedics, Thomas Jefferson University.

4 Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, The Rothman Institute at Thomas Jefferson University, Philadelphia, PA.

item.title: Deep vein blood clots 12X more frequent in SCI patients who did not get vitamin D supplements
